same...

cetera.
efficient, PgVector, But…

That's why we decided to do a reduction in the vectors, just to save some disk size.
With a PCM, you know, this was a really good solution.

We did these figures that just tried to find the right balance between the vector size and the accuracy of each of the queries based on the index, because that was another variable to take into account, the index that we were using.
Integer embeddings, binary embeddings, 16-bit half-precision embeddings, and stuff like that.
The first version, basically, we did this PCN reduction that was not enough because the index was huge in size.

I was trying to, I think that was the last version, reducing the embeddings and also the index.

Index.
